description: Placeholder — run fetch_adoption.py or trigger the GitHub Actions workflow to populate with live data.

government_procurement.description: Government AI contract awards and procurement notices.

US.note: Data not yet fetched. Trigger the 'Update Adoption Data' GitHub Actions workflow.

China.note: Data not yet fetched. CCGP may be inaccessible from non-Chinese IPs.

company_filings.description: Public company annual filings mentioning "AI deployment" (exact phrase) via SEC EDGAR.

US.note: Data not yet fetched. Trigger the 'Update Adoption Data' GitHub Actions workflow.

China_proxy.note: 20-F filings = all foreign private issuers on US exchanges (primarily Chinese ADRs, not exclusively China). Data not yet fetched.

usaspending.name: USASpending.gov
usaspending.note: Official US federal spending data. No API key required.

ccgp.name: CCGP (中国政府采购网)
ccgp.note: China central government procurement portal. Best-effort HTML scraping.

edgar.name: SEC EDGAR Full-Text Search (EFTS)
edgar.note: Public full-text search across SEC filings. No API key required.

methodology_note: Two-signal proxy: (1) government procurement actions (binding institutional decisions to acquire AI) and (2) public company annual filing mentions of AI deployment. US data is substantially more automatable and transparent than China data.
caveats: 1. Scope asymmetry: US = federal + SEC-listed companies. China = central CCGP (often blocked from US IPs) + foreign ADR proxy (non-exclusive). 2. CCGP null = inaccessible, not zero procurement. 3. 20-F proxy overstates China (covers all foreign issuers). 4. Counts reflect orders/mentions, not deployment outcomes.
